cta.title: Move Forward With Natural, Personalized Care
cta.subtitle: A consultation is an excellent first step. We’ll discuss your health goals, review which treatments may be the right fit, and create a personalized care plan tailored to your needs.

faqs.answer: Most people tolerate acupuncture very well and feel little to no discomfort. The needles are extremely thin—much finer than those used for injections—so insertion usually causes only a brief, mild sensation, if anything. Once the needles are in place, many patients feel deeply relaxed, and some even fall asleep during treatment.
faqs.question: Does acupuncture hurt?

faqs.answer: The number of treatments varies based on your condition, how long it has been present, and how your body responds. Acute issues may begin to improve within 3–6 visits, while chronic concerns often benefit from 10–15 sessions or more. From the beginning, we’ll outline a personalized treatment plan and adjust it over time based on your progress.
faqs.question: How many treatments will I need?

faqs.answer: Chinese medicine is generally considered safe when provided by a qualified, licensed practitioner. We use sterile, single-use needles and follow strict safety procedures during every treatment. Chinese herbal medicine has been refined over thousands of years, and we use only tested, high-quality herbs from trusted suppliers. When side effects do occur, they are typically mild and uncommon.
faqs.question: Is Chinese medicine safe?

faqs.answer: Yes. TCM can be used alongside Western medicine and often complements conventional care well. Many patients choose acupuncture to help with medication or procedure-related side effects, support recovery, or address issues that have not fully improved with standard treatment alone. We encourage clear communication with all of your healthcare providers so your care stays coordinated.
faqs.question: Can TCM be used together with Western medical care?

faqs.answer: Wear loose, comfortable clothing that can be rolled up easily so we can access areas such as the arms, legs, and back. If needed, gowns are available. We also ask that you avoid perfume and strongly scented products on the day of treatment, since some patients are sensitive to fragrance.
faqs.question: What should I wear for my treatment appointment?

faqs.answer: We accept most major insurance plans that include acupuncture benefits. If your plan requires you to submit claims yourself, we can provide a detailed superbill for possible reimbursement. We suggest contacting us before your appointment so we can help confirm your coverage. If you are paying out of pocket, we also offer self-pay rates and package options.
faqs.question: Do you accept insurance?

faqs.answer: Eat a light meal about one to two hours before your visit, since coming in on an empty stomach is not recommended. On the day of treatment, try to avoid alcohol and too much caffeine. Wear comfortable clothing, and bring a list of any medications or supplements you take. Your first visit will include a detailed health history, so you’re welcome to bring any questions you have.
faqs.question: How should I prepare for my first appointment?

faqs.answer: It’s very common to feel nervous about needles, and many patients are surprised by how comfortable acupuncture can be. The needles are very fine, and insertion is usually painless. We’ll go at a pace that feels comfortable for you, explain each step clearly, and can start with fewer needles if that helps. We also provide needle-free options, including herbal medicine, cupping, and Tui Na massage.
faqs.question: What if I’m nervous about needles?

overview.introduction: Gang Shi Acupuncture offers a wide range of Traditional Chinese Medicine treatments, each selected and tailored to your individual concerns and wellness goals. Our care combines long-established practices with modern clinical understanding to provide natural, whole-person support that focuses on the underlying patterns behind your concerns, not only the symptoms.

items.title: Licensed Providers
items.description: Treatment guided by evidence-informed clinical practice

items.title: Personalized Treatment Plans
items.description: Each plan is tailored to your individual health needs and goals

items.title: Safe and Professional Care
items.description: High standards for hygiene and professionalism at every visit

legacyLabels.whatToExpectTitle: What to Expect From Your Visit

items.title: Acupuncture Therapy
items.whatToExpect: At the start of your acupuncture session, you’ll be made comfortable while your practitioner places very fine needles at selected points. Most patients feel little to no discomfort. The needles are then left in place for about 20 to 30 minutes while you rest quietly, and many people experience a deep sense of calm. Some patients even fall asleep during treatment.
items.fullDescription:
Acupuncture involves the precise insertion of needles at specific points along the body’s meridians to support the balance of Yin and Yang and promote the smooth flow of Qi and blood. 

**Scope of Application**
This therapy may be used for acute and chronic pain conditions, including neck, shoulder, back, and leg pain, headaches, and arthritis; internal medicine concerns such as insomnia, gastrointestinal disorders, and hypertension; surgical and soft tissue issues including soft tissue injuries and skin conditions; gynecological concerns such as irregular menstruation and menopausal syndrome; pediatric conditions including indigestion and low immunity; men’s health concerns including prostate enlargement, impotence, premature ejaculation, and sexual dysfunction; and aesthetic TCM applications including acne management, anti-aging, weight management, skin lifting, and edema reduction. 

**Precautions**
Please do not receive acupuncture on an empty stomach or right after a large meal. Extra caution is advised for those who are pregnant or who have severe heart disease or bleeding disorders. Be sure to tell your acupuncturist if you take anti-platelet or anticoagulant medications. After treatment, keep warm, avoid exposure to cold drafts, and wait at least 4 hours before bathing.
items.shortDescription: One of the foundations of our care, acupuncture uses fine needles to help rebalance the body and support its natural healing response.

items.title: Traditional Chinese Herbal Medicine
benefits: Customized to match your individual health needs
Addresses underlying patterns, not just surface symptoms
Typically well tolerated with few side effects
Well suited for ongoing and chronic conditions
Helps maintain healthy internal organ function
items.whatToExpect: Care begins with a detailed consultation and TCM assessment. Based on that evaluation, we create a herbal formula tailored to your specific needs and provide clear instructions for preparing and taking it. As treatment continues, we follow up regularly and adjust the formula as needed to support your progress.
items.fullDescription:
Chinese Herbal Medicine uses carefully chosen herbal formulas to regulate the Yin, Yang, Qi, and blood of the organs according to TCM syndrome differentiation. 

**Scope of Application**
It may be used for internal medicine conditions, including stomach complaints, liver disease, and cardiovascular disease; gynecological concerns such as menstrual disorders, infertility, and menopause; pediatric issues including colds and indigestion; men’s health concerns including kidney deficiency and impotence; recovery after chronic illness; and anti-aging and wellness support. 

**Precautions**
Herbal formulas should always be taken under the supervision of a qualified TCM practitioner. If you are using Western medications, please let your practitioner know so the prescription can be adjusted appropriately. Pregnant women and individuals with special health considerations should follow their practitioner’s guidance closely.
items.shortDescription: Custom herbal formulas prepared to address the root of your health concerns while supporting your body’s natural healing process.

items.title: Therapeutic Cupping
items.whatToExpect: During treatment, your practitioner places glass or silicone cups on areas of tightness or discomfort. Most people describe the sensation as a firm, relieving pull rather than pain. The cups may remain in place for 5 to 15 minutes or be moved gently across the skin. Temporary round marks can appear afterward, which is normal and usually fades within a few days.
items.fullDescription:
Cupping therapy applies negative pressure to draw cups firmly against the skin, expanding the capillaries beneath to encourage blood flow, clear stagnation, and help the body expel wind and cold. 

**Scope of Application**
Colds, coughs, asthma, rheumatic joint pain, and muscle tension in the neck, shoulders, and back arising from wind-cold pathogens. 

**Precautions**
Cupping is not appropriate over broken or irritated skin, for those with bleeding disorders, or over the abdominal and lower back areas of pregnant women. Elderly patients should proceed with care. Following treatment, protect yourself from cold exposure, avoid overexertion, and wait before bathing to prevent wind-cold from entering through the pores.
items.shortDescription: A traditional therapy that uses suction cups to relieve muscle tension, promote circulation, and support the body’s natural recovery process.

items.title: Moxibustion Therapy
benefits: Helps support and strengthen immune function
Supports energy levels and overall vitality
items.whatToExpect: During a moxibustion session, you’ll feel a gentle, soothing warmth as burning moxa is held near selected points on the body. Many patients find the experience calming, grounding, and deeply relaxing, often comparing it to the soft warmth of sunlight on the skin. The herb also has a distinct earthy aroma that many people find comforting.
items.fullDescription:
Moxibustion uses the therapeutic heat created by burning moxa leaves near specific acupuncture points to warm the meridians, stimulate those points, and nourish the body’s vital energy. 

**Scope of Application**
It may be used for deficiency-cold constitutions, menstrual cramps, chronic gastrointestinal conditions, rheumatic pain, asthma, general fatigue, and cold hands or feet. 

**Precautions**
Treatment must be performed carefully to avoid burns. Moxibustion is not appropriate for individuals with a high fever or constitutions marked by excess heat. People with diabetic neuropathy should use extreme caution. Pregnant women should avoid treatment over the abdominal and sacral areas.
items.shortDescription: A gentle heat-based therapy using dried mugwort to support vitality, promote circulation, and help the body dispel cold.

items.title: Tui Na Therapeutic Massage
benefits: Helps relieve muscle pain and long-standing tension
Supports better posture and structural alignment
May help lessen how often headaches and migraines occur, as well as their intensity
items.whatToExpect: Tui Na may feel gentle and calming or more active and stimulating, depending on your condition and constitution. Your practitioner uses a variety of hands-on techniques with the hands, fingers, elbows, and sometimes feet. You remain fully clothed during the session, which usually lasts 30 to 60 minutes.
items.fullDescription:
Tui Na uses skilled manual techniques along the body’s meridians, muscles, and joints to regulate Qi and blood, relax the tendons, and improve circulation in specific areas. 

**Scope of Application**
This therapy may be used for neck, shoulder, back, and leg pain, muscle strain, sports injuries, indigestion, and pediatric conditions such as cough or constipation. 

**Precautions**
Tui Na should not be performed over open wounds, active skin infections, or areas affected by bone tumors. Deep abdominal pressure is not appropriate during pregnancy. For elderly patients and those with osteoporosis, only gentle techniques should be used.
items.shortDescription: A traditional Chinese medical massage that combines acupressure, assisted stretching, and joint mobilization in a single therapeutic treatment.

items.title: Gua Sha Therapy
benefits: May help the body recover more quickly after illness
Helps ease tension in the neck and shoulders
items.whatToExpect: At the start of treatment, your practitioner applies oil to the skin and then uses a smooth-edged tool with firm downward strokes over areas of tightness or discomfort. You will feel clear pressure and scraping during the session, which can be intense but is usually well tolerated. It is common for red or purplish marks to appear as treatment progresses, and these typically fade within 2 to 7 days.
items.fullDescription:
Gua Sha uses a smooth tool and repeated friction on the skin to promote circulation, open the meridians, and bring stagnant blood to the surface as part of supporting the body's response to pathogens. 

**Scope of Application**
It is commonly used for colds, fever, sore throat, heatstroke, hypertension, and tension or stiffness affecting the neck, shoulders, and back. 

**Precautions**
Avoid using this therapy over broken skin or moles. It is not suitable for people with bleeding disorders, and it should not be performed on the abdomen during pregnancy. After treatment, keep the area warm and covered, and avoid exposure to cold drafts or bathing immediately afterward.
items.shortDescription: A traditional scraping therapy used to relieve muscle tightness, encourage circulation, and help calm inflammation.

items.title: Herbal Patch Therapy
items.fullDescription:
Individually prepared herbal pastes or powders are placed directly on selected acupoints or areas of pain so the medicinal ingredients can be absorbed through the skin. 

**Scope of Application**
This approach is often used for rheumatic joint pain, ongoing waist and leg pain, asthma, rhinitis, pediatric cough conditions such as San-Fu-Tie, and various skin concerns. 

**Precautions**
Use care on sensitive skin. Do not apply to broken skin or open wounds. If marked redness, swelling, or itching occurs, stop use immediately.
items.shortDescription: Customized herbal applications placed on specific areas to support care through transdermal absorption.

items.title: Wellness & Lifestyle Counseling
benefits: Meaningful health changes that can be sustained over time
Useful strategies to better manage stress
Improved energy and a stronger sense of vitality
Preventive support aimed at lowering the likelihood of future illness
Support for improved overall treatment results
Individualized guidance for maintaining long-term wellness
items.whatToExpect: As part of your treatment plan, we look closely at key parts of your daily routine and provide practical recommendations you can use in everyday life. Guidance may include breathing techniques, meditation, sleep habits, seasonal routine changes, and ways to respond to stress more effectively. We work with you to create changes that feel manageable, personal, and sustainable.
items.fullDescription:
Long-term wellness depends on more than what happens during clinic visits.

Our lifestyle counseling takes a broader view of your health, including sleep, physical activity, stress patterns, and the habits that shape your day-to-day life.

Each recommendation is based on TCM principles and tailored to your individual constitution, needs, and circumstances.
items.shortDescription: Personalized support for sleep, movement, stress, and daily habits to help strengthen your long-term wellbeing.
